answer: The Greater Tokyo Area had 41 million residents in 2024, making it the world's most populous metropolitan area. The official metro population was 37,115,000 in 2024, with a slight annual decline. For precise figures, refer to official statistics.

results.title: Tokyo, Japan Metro Area Population (1950-2026) - Macrotrends
results.snippet: ## Tokyo, Japan Metro Area Population (1950-2026). ##### The current metro area population of Tokyo in 2026 is **36,954,000**, a **0.22% decline** from 2025. * The metro area population of Tokyo in 2025 was **37,036,000**, a **0.21% decline** from 2024. * The metro area population of Tokyo in 2024 was **37,115,000**, a **0.21% decline** from 2023. * The metro area population of Tokyo in 2023 was **37,194,000**, a **0.21% decline** from 2022. The full historical dataset is available for download here: [Tokyo, Japan Metro Population | Historical Data | 1950-2026](https://www.macrotrends.net/datasets/global-metrics/cities/21671/tokyo/population). ## Total Population.

results.title: Japan Diary日本
results.snippet: # Japan Diary日本 - Tokyo, Japan, is the world's most populous... ### **Japan Diary日本**. Tokyo, Japan, is the world's most populous metropolitan area, with over 37 million residents, yet it remains one of the safest cities globally. We lived in Western Japan for 10 years and visited Tokyo once or twice a year for a week each time. I’m now 51 years of age and have been blessed to travel the world extensively in my life and I’ll still tell anyone today that my favorite country on planet earth is Japan. Back home I’m a bit of a loner, yet in Japan I have so many friends. We lived in Western Japan for 10 years and visited Tokyo once or twice a year for a week each time. Back home I’m a bit of a loner, yet in Japan I have so many friends.
